crates.name: zk-core
crates.description: Pure cryptographic math. No Soroban dependencies. Compiles to no_std.

items.name: encrypt
items.doc: Stub for the encrypt function the contract is calling.
items.signature: pub fn encrypt( amount: u256, _pub_key: &G1Affine, _ephemeral: u256, ) -> Result<Self, ZkError>

items.name: decrypt_amount_point
items.doc: Stub for decryption that returns the mocked amount point
items.signature: pub fn decrypt_amount_point(&self, _private_key: u256) -> Result<G1Affine, ZkError>

items.name: scalar_mul
items.doc: Bridges the contract's method call to the Bn254 implementation.
items.signature: pub fn scalar_mul(&self, scalar: u256) -> G1Affine

items.name: to_affine
items.doc: Converts the projective point back to affine coordinates.

items.name: SafeFrom
items.doc: Constant-time, fallible conversion into a cryptographic type.

items.name: Bn254
items.doc: The BN254 elliptic curve group parameters and arithmetic operations.

items.name: BASE_MODULUS
items.doc: BN254 scalar field modulus r (order of G1/G2).

items.name: sub
items.signature: pub fn sub(a: u256, b: u256) -> u256

items.name: is_valid_fq
items.doc: Validates a BN254 base field element in Fq.  This ensures the element is within the field modulus and prevents malformed G2 coordinate components from being passed into the native host pairing call.

items.name: add
items.signature: pub fn add(a: u256, b: u256) -> u256

items.name: mul
items.signature: pub fn mul(a: u256, b: u256) -> u256

items.name: pow
items.signature: pub fn pow(base: u256, exp: u256) -> u256

items.name: mul_fq
items.signature: pub fn mul_fq(a: u256, b: u256) -> u256

items.name: add_fq
items.signature: pub fn add_fq(a: u256, b: u256) -> u256

items.name: sub_fq
items.signature: pub fn sub_fq(a: u256, b: u256) -> u256

items.name: is_valid_g1
items.signature: pub fn is_valid_g1(x: u256, y: u256) -> bool

items.name: is_valid_g1_subgroup
items.signature: pub fn is_valid_g1_subgroup(x: u256, y: u256) -> bool

items.name: g1_scalar_mul
items.signature: pub fn g1_scalar_mul(point: G1Projective, scalar: u256) -> G1Projective

items.name: ct_select
items.signature: pub fn ct_select(choice: u128, a: Self, b: Self) -> Self

items.name: double
items.doc: Doubles the projective point (2 * P) using Jacobian formulas.

items.name: evaluate
items.signature: pub fn evaluate(&self, x: u256) -> u256

items.name: try_mul
items.signature: pub fn try_mul(&self, other: &Self) -> Result<Self, ZkError>

items.name: from_terms_slice
items.signature: pub fn from_terms_slice(terms: &[(usize, u256)]) -> Result<Self, ZkError>

crates.name: zk-soroban
crates.description: Stellar integration. Traits that extend the Soroban Env, host-function mappings, and XDR conversion.

items.name: round_constants
items.doc: Return the cached BN254 Poseidon2 round constants, computing and storing them on the first call within the contract.

items.name: mat_diag
items.doc: Return the cached BN254 Poseidon2 matrix diagonal, computing and storing it on the first call within the contract.

items.name: fr_modulus
items.doc: Return the cached BN254 Fr modulus, computing and storing it on the first call within the contract.

items.name: validate_soroban_scalar
items.doc: Validates a Soroban U256 as a BN254 scalar. This prevents "out of bounds" field element errors in ZK verifiers.
items.signature: pub fn validate_soroban_scalar(_env: &Env, val: U256) -> bool

items.name: ZkEnv
items.doc: Helper trait to add this functionality directly to the Env

items.name: HostConvert
items.doc: Zero-copy conversion from a Soroban host-managed [`U256`] into a validated BN254 [`Fr`] field element.  This trait is designed to wrap the `env.crypto().bn254_fr_from_u256()` host call when it becomes available as a native Soroban API.  The current implementation performs the conversion in software via big-endian byte mapping with no heap allocation, then delegates range validation to [`Fr::safe_from`].

items.name: validate_scalar
items.doc: Benchmark function to ensure CI measures REAL library footprint.
items.signature: pub fn validate_scalar(env: Env, val: U256) -> bool

items.name: poseidon2_hash
items.doc: Poseidon2 hash of a list of BN254 field elements, using the instance-storage cache for the round constants and matrix diagonal (Issue #124).  The first invocation populates the cache from code; later invocations reuse the constants stored in `StorageType::Instance` instead of rebuilding them on every call.
items.signature: pub fn poseidon2_hash(env: Env, inputs: soroban_sdk::Vec<U256>) -> U256

items.name: to_bytes
items.doc: Serializes the G2 point into a 128-byte array according to CAP-0074 §3.2 / EIP-197.  ## Byte Layout The 128 bytes are structured as: - Bytes 0..32:   `x.1` (X imaginary / c1) - Bytes 32..64:  `x.0` (X real / c0) - Bytes 64..96:  `y.1` (Y imaginary / c1) - Bytes 96..128: `y.0` (Y real / c0)  All 32-byte chunks are encoded in Big-Endian format.

items.name: pairing_check
items.doc: Evaluates the BN254 pairing check e(A1, B1) * ... * e(An, Bn) == 1.
items.signature: pub fn pairing_check(env: &Env, pairs: &[(G1Affine, G2Affine)]) -> Result<bool, ZkError>

items.name: Poseidon2Sponge
items.doc: Poseidon2 sponge over BN254 Fr (t=3, rate=2, capacity=1).  Directly calls `env.crypto_hazmat().poseidon2_permutation()` so the permutation runs as a single host call — no guest-side loop overhead.  # Example ```ignore let mut sponge = Poseidon2Sponge::new(&env); sponge.absorb(&inputs); let digest = sponge.squeeze(); ```

items.name: new
items.doc: Create a new sponge with zeroed state, building the BN254 constants from code (no contract storage required).  The round constants and matrix diagonal are computed once here and reused across every [`Poseidon2Sponge::absorb`]/[`Poseidon2Sponge::squeeze`] permutation, rather than being rebuilt on each permutation.

items.name: new_cached
items.doc: Create a new sponge whose BN254 constants are sourced from the contract instance storage cache (Issue #124).  On the first invocation within a contract the constants are computed and written to `StorageType::Instance`; subsequent invocations read them back from storage instead of rebuilding them. Must be called from within a contract invocation context (it touches instance storage).

items.name: absorb
items.doc: Absorb a slice of BN254 Fr field elements into the sponge.

items.name: squeeze
items.doc: Squeeze one field element.  Pads and applies the permutation if any unprocessed input remains, then returns the first element of the rate portion.

items.name: hash_to_field
items.doc: Hash a slice of BN254 Fr field elements to a single field element.  Compatible with Noir/Circom Poseidon2 constraints (t=3, d=5, rate=2). Uses capacity-zero sponge initialisation.

items.name: hash_to_field_cached
items.doc: Instance-cached variant of [`hash_to_field`] (Issue #124).  Identical output to [`hash_to_field`], but the BN254 round constants and matrix diagonal are loaded from (and lazily populated into) the contract instance storage cache rather than rebuilt from code. Must be called from within a contract invocation context.

items.name: Transcript
items.doc: Fiat-Shamir transcript built on top of the BN254 Poseidon2 sponge.  The transcript is stateful: each absorb updates the sponge state, and each squeeze advances the permutation before producing the next challenge.

items.name: squeeze_challenge
items.doc: Squeeze the next Fiat-Shamir challenge as a validated BN254 `Fr`.
items.signature: pub fn squeeze_challenge(&mut self) -> Result<Fr, ZkError>
